subject: Computer Networking
title: Modulation Techniques
description: A short lesson to help you apply Modulation Techniques.

hook.text: A radio wave is just a wave. To carry data, you have to change something about it — that's modulation.

buildup.text: AM changes the wave's height. FM changes its frequency. Digital modulation combines both for maximum data density.

discovery.text: QAM — Quadrature Amplitude Modulation — encodes multiple bits per symbol by varying both amplitude and phase.

twist.text: Higher-order QAM packs more bits per symbol but needs a cleaner signal. Noise kills complex modulation schemes.

climax.text: Wi-Fi 6 uses 1024-QAM, encoding 10 bits per symbol. That's why modern Wi-Fi is so much faster than older versions.

punchline.text: Modulation turns waves into data carriers. More complex, more speed.

quiz.question: What does QAM modulate to encode data?
options: Both the amplitude and phase of the signal
Only the frequency of the signal
The physical length of the antenna
